description: Mining Guardian policy rules for warehouse pilot environment

rules.name: Miner must be online
rules.note: Immediately notify operator via flag_for_operator_review. Do not auto-restart offline miners until connectivity and power state are confirmed manually.

rules.name: Hashrate below warning threshold
rules.note: Passing condition: hashrate must be >= model minimum. Violation fires when hashrate falls below model-specific minimum floor.

rules.name: Hashrate critically low
rules.note: Passing condition: hashrate must be >= 80% of model target. Violation fires when hashrate falls below that threshold. Restart is allowed only after checking maintenance mode and recent reboot history.

rules.name: Chip temperature warning
rules.note: Passing condition: chip_temp must be <= environment warning max. Violation fires when chip_temp exceeds that threshold. Profile reduction is preferred before restart.

rules.name: Chip temperature critical
rules.note: Passing condition: chip_temp must be <= environment safe max. Violation fires when chip_temp exceeds that threshold. Low-risk automatic thermal protection action.

rules.name: Board temperature warning
rules.note: Passing condition: board_temp must be <= environment warning max. Violation fires when board_temp exceeds that threshold. Only applies to environments that expose board temperature thresholds (immersion).

rules.name: Immersion inlet temperature exceeded
rules.note: Passing condition: inlet_temp must be <= environment inlet max. Elevated inlet temperature indicates a cooling infrastructure issue upstream of the miner. Miner-level action cannot resolve this — operator must investigate chiller or fluid supply.

rules.name: Immersion outlet temperature exceeded
rules.note: Passing condition: outlet_temp must be <= environment outlet max. High outlet temperature indicates insufficient cooling capacity or overloaded miner. Switch to safe profile to reduce heat output. Cooling infrastructure should be investigated in parallel.

rules.name: Hydro coolant inlet temperature exceeded
rules.note: Passing condition: coolant_inlet_temp must be <= environment coolant inlet max. Indicates chiller or coolant supply side issue. Cannot be resolved by miner-level action — operator must investigate cooling loop.

rules.name: Hydro coolant outlet temperature exceeded
rules.note: Passing condition: coolant_outlet_temp must be <= environment coolant outlet max. High outlet temperature indicates excessive heat rejection from the miner. Switch to safe profile to reduce load. Investigate coolant flow rate and chiller capacity.

rules.name: Primary pool must match approved list
rules.note: Never auto-change wallet or pool destination without explicit approval.

rules.name: Backup pool must be enabled
rules.note: Any pool configuration change requires operator approval for consistency with pool_config_approved protection. Low-risk but kept under the same approval gate as all pool-related changes.

rules.name: Active profile must be approved for model
rules.note: Profile should be one of the approved profiles for the miner model.

rules.name: Firmware version must be approved
rules.note: Detect drift first. Do not auto-upgrade firmware in pilot phase.

rules.name: Restart count in last 24 hours
rules.note: approval_required is intentionally false — this rule only raises a notification, it does not trigger a remediating action. Frequent restarts often indicate instability, power issues, or cooling issues and require human judgment.

rules.name: Hardware error rate threshold
rules.note: Avoid aggressive clocks if hardware errors rise.

rules.name: Hydro flow minimum per machine
rules.note: Particularly important for Auradine hydro deployments where flow requirement is unusually high. Flow threshold is defined at the environment level (environments.hydro.flow_min_lpm_per_machine).

rules.name: Do not remediate during maintenance mode
rules.note: All corrective actions should be skipped when maintenance mode is active.

investigate_connectivity.description: Check power, switch port, IP reachability, AMS connectivity, and miner responsiveness before any restart.

reapply_last_stable_profile.description: Reapply most recent known-good profile from AMS history.

switch_to_balanced_profile.description: Move miner to balanced operating profile.

switch_to_safe_profile.description: Move miner to conservative thermal-safe profile.

restore_approved_pool_config.description: Restore approved primary and backup pool configuration from policy baseline.

enable_backup_pool.description: Enable configured backup pool in miner settings.

schedule_firmware_review.description: Create an operator task to review firmware version drift and schedule maintenance window if needed.

flag_for_operator_review.description: Raise alert in the operations dashboard or notifier channel for human review.

skip_actions.description: Do not apply remediation while maintenance mode is active.
